title: Peer review verdict (one iteration, the summative grade)
description: What the single fresh peer_reviewer returns each iteration. It grades the paper on the same 5 axes the judges critique, but INDEPENDENTLY — it re-derives each axis from the paper + dataset, treats the judges' critiques as hypotheses it may overturn, and verifies a sample of claims/numbers/citations itself. One file per iteration at <sandbox_root>/iter<N>/peer_review.json.

axes.description: One entry per axis. Omit 'code' entirely when no code produces plots/results (n_axes drops to 4).

overall_score.description: 100 × (Σ axis scores) / (5 × n_axes). n_axes = 5, or 4 when 'code' is absent. Equal weight, no per-axis weighting.

gate_failures.description: Confirmed hard-gate violations that force pass=false regardless of overall_score: code that contradicts a stated result, a fabricated/incorrect citation, or a figure whose numbers != the text. The reviewer must independently confirm each before listing it.

issues_judges_missed.description: Real problems the reviewer found that none of the 5 judges raised. Required evidence of independent reading; an empty array is allowed only if the reviewer genuinely found none after looking.

spotchecks.description: Independent verifications the reviewer performed this round (NOT trusting that a 'fixed' item was fixed).

target.description: What was checked, e.g. 'Table 1 mean = dataset col B mean' or 'citation [12] supports the claim it is attached to'.

method.description: How, e.g. 'recomputed from data/results.csv', 'lit_search snippet on [12]'.

substance_check.description: Goodhart guard: did this iteration's revisions add real substance, or only surface compliance (padded prose, cosmetic figure tweaks that don't fix the message)? Brief judgment.

score.description: Anchored 1-5 (see roles/peer_reviewer.md). 5 = publishable-quality on this axis; 1 = severely deficient.

justification.description: Why this score, grounded in specifics — the reviewer's own assessment, not a restatement of the judge.

evidence.description: file:line, figure path, quoted number, or citation the score rests on.

blocking_issues.description: Any confirmed block-level defects on this axis (also surfaced in gate_failures).
